Jacobs went under 10 seconds for the first time this season in Finland

Removed from bookmarks

I would like to be emailed about offers, events and updates from The Independent. Read ourPrivacy notice

Olympic100m championLamont Marcell Jacobsramped up his preparations forParis 2024by breaching the 10-second mark twice on Tuesday to win gold at the Paavo Nurmi Games in Finland.

The Italian, whoclocked 9.80s in the Tokyo finaland also won 4x100m relay gold, finished top of his heat in Turku with a time of 9.99s before claiming victory in the final in 9.92s.

Prior to Tuesday, the 29-year-old had dipped under 10 seconds just oncesince his Tokyo Games triumphin 2021. His time in Tuesday’s final was the third-fastest of his career.

“Before today I was a bit worried that I hadn’t run under 10 seconds yet (this season), but it’s part of the game and now I have done it twice,” Jacobs said.

“In the heats, I had a really good start, the last part was average. In the final, it was the other way around, so now we need to put the pieces together.”

The season’s leading time is 9.79s, set by Ferdinand Omanyala on Saturday at Kenya’s Olympic trials. The athletics competition at the Paris Olympics takes place from 1-11 August at the Stade de France.

title: Olympic champion Lamont Marcell Jacobs clocks fastest 100m time since Tokyo | The Independent
description: Jacobs went under 10 seconds for the first time this season in Finland
